title: ArchiveBox
summary: ArchiveBox teaches COMPREHENSIVE multi-format preservation from a single URL.  One input URL → HTML, singlefile HTML, screenshot PNG, PDF, WARC, title, article text, favicon, headers, git clone, MP3/MP4, subtitles.
creator_view: ArchiveBox teaches COMPREHENSIVE multi-format preservation from a single URL. One input URL → HTML, singlefile HTML, screenshot PNG, PDF, WARC, title, article text, favicon, headers, git clone, MP3/MP4, subtitles. Uses standard tools (Chrome, wget, yt-dlp, git). Detects content type and extracts ALL formats simultaneously. The methodology insight: don't choose one output format — preserve everything, let the consumer pick. Also handles social media (posts, comments, images), YouTube (video, subtitles, metadata), and GitHub (git clone, README). Browser extension, CLI, REST API, webhooks.
creatorLens: ArchiveBox teaches COMPREHENSIVE multi-format preservation from a single URL. Detects content type and extracts ALL formats simultaneously. The methodology insight: don't choose one output format — preserve everything, let the consumer pick. Also handles social media (posts, comments, images), YouTube (video, subtitles, metadata), and GitHub (git clone, README). Browser extension, CLI, REST API, webhooks.

context.t28Relevance: Multi-format-per-URL extraction methodology. 'Preserve everything' approach. Social media, YouTube, GitHub extraction via standard tools. Input format flexibility (bookmarks, browser history, RSS, Pocket, Pinboard).
notablePatterns: One URL → many output formats simultaneously (HTML, PNG, PDF, WARC, text, media)
Content-type detection → appropriate extraction tools (Chrome for web, yt-dlp for video, git for repos)
Standard tools composition: Chrome + wget + yt-dlp + git + singlefile
Multiple input sources: bookmarks, browser history, RSS, Pocket, Pinboard, webhooks
Durable formats: everything stored as plain files readable without ArchiveBox
